title: Exotic Savory Algerian Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional Algerian dish featuring tender lamb, fresh flavors, and a festive atmosphere, with a cheese pull and al dente finish, made with ayurvedic rajasic friendly ingredients.
instructions: Heat oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a large Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned, stirring occasionally.
Add the lamb to the pot and cook until it is browned on all sides, stirring occasionally.
Add the garlic, ginger, cumin, coriander, and cayenne pepper to the pot and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chopped dried apricots, chicken broth, and honey to the pot and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender.
Stir in the crumbled feta cheese and cook until it is melted and the sauce is creamy.
Season the tagine with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot over couscous or rice.
Garnish with fresh parsley or cilantro, if desired.
